original_theorem:
If $\mathbf{X}$ and $\mathbf{Y}$ are
 in $\R^n,$ then
$$
|\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{Y}|\ge\left| |\mathbf{X}|-|\mathbf{Y}|\right|.
$$

We can assume the following statement(s) without a proof. 

If $\mathbf{X}$ and $\mathbf{Y}$ are in $\R^n,$ then
\begin{equation}\label{eq:5.1.6}
|\mathbf{X}+\mathbf{Y}|\le |\mathbf{X}|+|\mathbf{Y}|,
\end{equation}
with equality if and only if one of the vectors is a nonnegative
multiple of the other$.$
original_proof:
Since
$$
\mathbf{X}=\mathbf{Y}+(\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{Y}),
$$
Theorem~\ref{thmtype:5.1.6} implies that
$$
|\mathbf{X}|\le |\mathbf{Y}|+|\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{Y}|,
$$
which is equivalent to
$$
|\mathbf{X}|-|\mathbf{Y}|\le |\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{Y}|.
$$
Interchanging $\mathbf{X}$ and $\mathbf{Y}$  yields
$$
|\mathbf{Y}|-|\mathbf{X}|\le |\mathbf{Y}-\mathbf{X}|.
$$
Since $|\mathbf{X}-\mathbf{Y}|=|\mathbf{Y}-\mathbf{X}|$,
the last two inequalities imply the stated conclusion.
LLM_output:
```lean4
import Mathlib
import Aesop

set_option maxHeartbeats 0

open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat Filter

theorem reverse_triangle_inequality {n : ‚Ąē} (X Y : EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin n)) :
  ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ ‚Č• abs (‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ) := by
  -- The goal is equivalent to `|‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ| ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`.
  rw [ge_iff_le]
  -- We prove this by splitting it into two inequalities, using `abs_sub_le_iff`:
  -- 1. `‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`
  -- 2. `‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`
  apply abs_sub_le_iff.mpr
  constructor

  -- Proof of the first inequality: `‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`
  -- The informal proof derives this from `‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖY + (X-Y)‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖX-Y‚ÄĖ`.
  ¬∑ -- We rearrange our goal to `‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ`, which is easier to prove.
    rw [sub_le_iff_le_add]
    -- Now we show `‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ` using a `calc` block.
    calc
      ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖ(X - Y) + Y‚ÄĖ := by rw [sub_add_cancel X Y]
      _ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ := by apply norm_add_le (X - Y) Y

  -- Proof of the second inequality: `‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`
  -- The informal proof obtains this by interchanging X and Y in the argument
  -- for the first inequality, which gives `‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ`,
  -- and then uses the fact that `‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`.
  ¬∑ -- We want to show `‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ`.
    -- First, we use `‚ÄĖX - Y‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ to make the goal symmetric to the first case.
    rw [norm_sub_rev X Y]
    -- The goal is now `‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ - ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ`. We rearrange it to `‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ.
    rw [sub_le_iff_le_add]
    -- This follows from the triangle inequality, shown by the following `calc` block.
    calc
      ‚ÄĖY‚ÄĖ = ‚ÄĖ(Y - X) + X‚ÄĖ := by rw [sub_add_cancel Y X]
      _ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖY - X‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖX‚ÄĖ := by apply norm_add_le (Y - X) X
```
